title: Azure Release pipelines bash script deployment leads to Connection Refused.
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)** /kind bug **Description** I am using Azure Release Pipelines to pull an image and run a container based on that image, via a bash script. The container starts and responds normally from within the script, however if I login on the VM and try to access it, I get Connection Refused. **Steps to reproduce the issue:** 1. Create an Azure Deployment Group with a Linux VM (e.g. deploy01), and a Release Pipeline that uses it (https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/devops/pipelines/apps/cd/deploy-linuxvm-deploygroups?view=azure-devops&tabs=javascript) 2. In the bash script enter the following commands (minimal reproducible example -- no need to have artifacts etc.): ``` # Stop old container podman stop my-container || true podman rm my-container || true # Start new container podman run --name my-container -p 8000:80 -d nginx:alpine curl http://localhost:8000 # observe that the container responds normally ``` 3. Login to the deploy01 VM and enter `curl http://localhost:8000 -v` **Describe the results you received:** ``` * Rebuilt URL to: http://localhost:8000/ * Trying 127.0.0.1... * TCP_NODELAY set * connect to 127.0.0.1 port 8000 failed: Connection refused * Failed to connect to localhost port 8000: Connection refused * Closing connection 0 curl: (7) Failed to connect to localhost port 8000: Connection refused ``` **Describe the results you expected:** ``` * Rebuilt URL to: http://localhost:8000/ * Trying 127.0.0.1... * TCP_NODELAY set * Connected to localhost (127.0.0.1) port 8000 (#0) > GET / HTTP/1.1 > Host: localhost:8000 > User-Agent: curl/7.61.1 > Accept: */* > < HTTP/1.1 200 OK < Server: nginx/1.23.1 < Date: Sun, 11 Sep 2022 07:17:26 GMT < Content-Type: text/html < Content-Length: 615 < Last-Modified: Tue, 19 Jul 2022 15:23:19 GMT < Connection: keep-alive < ETag: "62d6cc67-267" < Accept-Ranges: bytes < Welcome to nginx!

Welcome to nginx!

If you see this page, the nginx web server is successfully installed and working. Further configuration is required.

For online documentation and support please refer to nginx.org.
Commercial support is available at nginx.com.

Thank you for using nginx.

* Connection #0 to host localhost left intact ``` **Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):** When logged in interactively to the VM with my user (same user that is running the Azure agent), if I restart the container with `podman restart `, I get the expected output. **Output of `podman version`:** ``` Client: Podman Engine Version: 4.1.1 API Version: 4.1.1 Go Version: go1.17.12 Built: Tue Aug 2 10:53:14 2022 OS/Arch: linux/amd64 ``` **Output of `podman info`:** ``` host: arch: amd64 buildahVersion: 1.26.2 cgroupControllers: [] cgroupManager: cgroupfs cgroupVersion: v1 conmon: package: conmon-2.1.2-2.module+el8.6.0+997+05c9d812.x86_64 path: /usr/bin/conmon version: 'conmon version 2.1.2, commit: 98e028a5804809ccb49bc099c0d53adc43ef8cc4' cpuUtilization: idlePercent: 99.59 systemPercent: 0.14 userPercent: 0.27 cpus: 4 distribution: distribution: '"rocky"' version: "8.6" eventLogger: file hostname: qa idMappings: gidmap: - container_id: 0 host_id: 1000 size: 1 - container_id: 1 host_id: 100000 size: 65536 uidmap: - container_id: 0 host_id: 1000 size: 1 - container_id: 1 host_id: 100000 size: 65536 kernel: 4.18.0-372.19.1.el8_6.x86_64 linkmode: dynamic logDriver: k8s-file memFree: 7413559296 memTotal: 8140410880 networkBackend: cni ociRuntime: name: runc package: runc-1.1.3-2.module+el8.6.0+997+05c9d812.x86_64 path: /usr/bin/runc version: |- runc version 1.1.3 spec: 1.0.2-dev go: go1.17.12 libseccomp: 2.5.2 os: linux remoteSocket: path: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock security: apparmorEnabled: false capabilities: CAP_NET_RAW,CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT rootless: true seccompEnabled: true seccompProfilePath: /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json selinuxEnabled: false serviceIsRemote: false slirp4netns: executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns package: slirp4netns-1.2.0-2.module+el8.6.0+997+05c9d812.x86_64 version: |- slirp4netns version 1.2.0 commit: 656041d45cfca7a4176f6b7eed9e4fe6c11e8383 libslirp: 4.4.0 SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 3 libseccomp: 2.5.2 swapFree: 0 swapTotal: 0 uptime: 15h 9m 28.47s (Approximately 0.62 days) plugins: log: - k8s-file - none - passthrough - journald network: - bridge - macvlan - ipvlan volume: - local registries: search: - registry.access.redhat.com - registry.redhat.io - docker.io store: configFile: /home//.config/containers/storage.conf containerStore: number: 4 paused: 0 running: 1 stopped: 3 graphDriverName: overlay graphOptions: {} graphRoot: /home//.local/share/containers/storage graphRootAllocated: 208235757568 graphRootUsed: 3582435328 graphStatus: Backing Filesystem: extfs Native Overlay Diff: "true" Supports d_type: "true" Using metacopy: "false" imageCopyTmpDir: /var/tmp imageStore: number: 3 runRoot: /run/user/1000/containers volumePath: /home//.local/share/containers/storage/volumes version: APIVersion: 4.1.1 Built: 1659426794 BuiltTime: Tue Aug 2 10:53:14 2022 GitCommit: "" GoVersion: go1.17.12 Os: linux OsArch: linux/amd64 Version: 4.1.1 ``` **Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):** ``` Rocky Linux 8 - AppStream 11 MB/s | 9.7 MB 00:00 Rocky Linux 8 - BaseOS 13 MB/s | 6.7 MB 00:00 Rocky Linux 8 - Extras 59 kB/s | 12 kB 00:00 Rocky Linux 8 - PowerTools 5.4 MB/s | 2.5 MB 00:00 Extra Packages for Enterprise Linux 8 - x86_64 11 MB/s | 13 MB 00:01 Extra Packages for Enterprise Linux Modular 8 - x86_64 781 kB/s | 734 kB 00:00 Installed Packages podman.x86_64 2:4.1.1-2.module+el8.6.0+997+05c9d812 @appstream ``` **Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)** Yes, the latest that is available in my distro as a package (4.1.1). **Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):** Rocky Linux VM hosted in Contabo.

comments.body:
Is your pipeline killing all spawned processes once it finishes?
Check if you have any rootlessport or slirp4netns process still running (assuming you run rootless).

I would guess so, yeah.  Not sure how else to check this, other than with `ps`, so here goes:
```
[root@qa ~]# ps aux | grep rootless
root       25871  0.0  0.0  12140  1196 pts/0    S+   21:24   0:00 grep --color=auto rootless
[root@qa ~]# ps aux | grep slirp
root       25893  0.0  0.0  12140  1088 pts/0    S+   21:26   0:00 grep --color=auto slirp
```
I logged on as root to the VM and ran the above commands. Looks like no `rootlessport` or `slirp4netns` processes are running.

ETA: I am running the default installation of docker/podman (`dnf install docker`) on my VM.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
For what it's worth, if I run these commands with `sudo` in my pipeline, everything works perfectly.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

As root we do not need user space proxy process, the ports are forwarded via iptables that is why it works.
Something is killing these processes.

I think you make a good point there. I did some more tests and indeed it seems that the Linux Azure agent terminates some processes critical to this task as it exits the deployment shell on the VM. I haven't looked into it in depth, to be honest, as I worked around this issue by using `root` to spin up my (non-prod) containers.

Since it is most probably not a Podman issue, but rather an Azure agent issue, I will close it for now, and update it if I have any newer info in the future.

Thanks for the help and insight @Luap99 !
